economic_notes: Nation has multiple government business enterprises including aquaculture, construction, forestry, land development, and tenure holding. Malahat Investment Corporation is the primary GBE with total assets of $30,795,059 and total liabilities of $38,103,853. Note receivable to Malahat Investment Corporation of $37,976,925 including interest. COVID-19 pandemic noted as subsequent event with unknown financial impact.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED - financial information for certain GBEs and partnerships not available and not accounted for in consolidated statements. 16 entities accounted for on modified equity basis (GBEs and partnerships). Long-term debt includes $39,695,851 in FNFA promissory notes plus $2,481,663 in All Nations Trust/other loans plus $23,645 capital lease. Annual debt service calculated as principal repayments on long-term debt ($123,652) plus promissory notes ($495,089). ISC Recovery of -$67,591 netted as negative revenue line. Total government transfers include ISC ($1,640,785), ISC Recovery (-$67,591), CMHC ($116,722), Fisheries and Oceans Canada ($299,840), First Nation Health Authority ($853,615), Natural Resources Canada ($108,874), HRSDC ($23,495), Ottawa Trust Fund ($6,182), Province of BC ($448,000), and Funding from First Nation Organizations ($272,152). Prior year figures restated for CMHC replacement reserve deferred revenue recognition. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text.
